questions: What are the principles relating to processing of personal data under the GDPR?
What is the right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')?
What is the maximum administrative fine under the GDPR?
Which artificial intelligence practices are prohibited under the AI Act?
How are high-risk AI systems classified under the AI Act?
ground_truth: Lawfulness, fairness and transparency; purpose limitation; data minimisation; accuracy; storage limitation; integrity and confidentiality; and accountability
The data subject has the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning them without undue delay
Up to 20 000 000 EUR, or up to 4% of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher
Practices such as subliminal manipulation, exploiting vulnerabilities, social scoring, and certain uses of biometric identification
By the criteria in Article 6 together with the use cases listed in Annex III
